name: MET-PREVENT did not improve walk speed and had tolerability concerns
summary: MET-PREVENT constrains metformin functional-benefit claims in older adults with probable sarcopenia and frailty risk.
statement: In MET-PREVENT, four months of metformin did not improve 4-meter walk speed versus placebo in older adults with probable sarcopenia and physical prefrailty or frailty, and the source concluded that metformin was poorly tolerated in this population.
population_or_model: Adults aged 65 years and older with probable sarcopenia and physical prefrailty or frailty
quantitative_note: Adjusted treatment effect on 4-meter walk speed was 0.001 m/s (95% CI -0.06 to 0.06; p=0.96); adverse events occurred in 35/35 metformin participants versus 33/36 placebo participants, and hospital admissions occurred in 12/35 versus 3/36.
caveats: The trial was small, with 72 randomized participants.
The population had established physical vulnerability, so results may not generalize to healthier adults.
Hospitalization imbalance should be interpreted cautiously but is material for safety review.
